page: Amazon Web Services m8azn.large is a general-purpose instance powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R05 processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at a high clock speed of 5.0 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. It runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded compression/decompression tasks scoring in the top 10%. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth fall into the average tier, reflecting the 2-core configuration. Ray tracing and PassMark memory scores are relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25%, which indicates a tradeoff when executing memory-intensive or highly parallelized graphical tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8azn.large offers a high-frequency compute profile for workloads that do not require massive parallelization but benefit from strong single-threaded execution. This makes it highly suitable for lightweight web serving, text processing, single-threaded batch processing, and basic database operations. It is less suitable for memory-heavy databases, large-scale LLM inference, or multi-threaded high-performance computing.
description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R05 processor running at 5.0 GHz. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, has no local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by high scores in single-threaded compression and Geekbench single-core benchmarks. Conversely, multi-core and memory-intensive benchmarks fall into average or weak categories due to the 2-core limit and memory throughput constraints. The m8azn.large is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads requiring high clock speeds on a limited budget, making it well-suited for single-threaded batch processing, text processing, and lightweight web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 dedicated 5.0 GHz AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for lightweight, high-frequency workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.large features 2 dedicated 5.0 GHz AMD EPYC vCPUs and 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, delivering top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: A high-frequency general-purpose instance featuring dedicated 5.0 GHz AMD processors and DDR5 memory for strong single-threaded performance.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9R05 processor running at 5.0 GHz
8.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz
Top-tier single-core and single-threaded compression performance
Baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps with Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for text processing, lightweight web serving, and single-threaded workloads
